perturbation.original_text: “ Confidential Information ” – Confidential Information is any information of the Discloser of actual or potential commercial value owing to the fact that it is unknown to t hird parties; disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient in writing, by way of document delivery, including in an electronic format safeguarded from unauthorised access to the information in transit; related to business or financial plans or strategies, f inancial reports or accounting (except to the extent contemplated by the law), contractual relations, pricing and marketing of goods, products or services, technical information, commercial secrets, know -how, research, production plans, concepts, intellect ual property items (including inventions, discoveries, innovative ideas, useful models, designs, production prototypes not covered by patent for whatever reason, computer programmes, databases, trademark designs not registered for whatever reason).
perturbation.changed_text: “ Confidential Information ” – Confidential Information is any information of the Discloser of actual commercial value owing to the fact that it is unknown to t hird parties; disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient in writing, by way of document delivery, including in an electronic format safeguarded from unauthorised access to the information in transit; related to business or financial plans or strategies, f inancial reports or accounting (except to the extent contemplated by the law), contractual relations, pricing and marketing of goods, products or services, technical information, commercial secrets, know -how, research, production plans, concepts, intellect ual property items (including inventions, discoveries, innovative ideas, useful models, designs, production prototypes not covered by patent for whatever reason, computer programmes, databases, trademark designs not registered for whatever reason).
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'or potential' is removed from the definition of 'Confidential Information.' This now implies that information with only *potential* commercial value is no longer protected under the NDA. This directly contradicts the purpose of the NDA, which is to protect a wide range of business data, especially information that could become valuable in the future.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Discloser will disclose to the Recipient confidential information related to implementation of a service for receiving compensation for delayed and canceled flights.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes "or potential commercial value" in the definition of Confidential Information, while the changed text removes "or potential". The contradicted text states that the Discloser will disclose confidential information related to implementation of a service. Because the 'potential' aspect has been removed, the recipient may argue that information disclosed by the discloser which has potential commercial value but not 'actual' commercial value, related to the implementation of service, is not considered confidential, hence it is not protected. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction because what is considered confidential is narrowed significantly by the change, conflicting with the contradicted text, which indicates confidential information will be disclosed.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall keep Discloser’s Confidentia l Information confidential and use Confidential Information only for the above -stated Purpose of Disclosure, or for performing its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information internally wit hin its organization (including Affiliates) only on a “need -to-know” basis, and except for Cardholder Information which shall not be disclosed, to its outside attorneys, consultants, contractors and sub -contractors (“ Representatives ”), only to the extent t hose individuals are bound by a legal obligation or agreement that is as protective of the Confidential Information as 2 this Agreement. A Recipient is fully responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Affiliates or Representative s. A Recipient shall use the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as it uses to prevent unauthorized disclosure of its own Confidential Information. Except for the permitted uses and disclosures expressly set out in this Agree ment, no other uses or disclosures are permitted.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall keep Discloser’s Confidentia l Information confidential and use Confidential Information only for the above -stated Purpose of Disclosure, or for performing its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information internally wit hin its organization (including Affiliates) and its Representatives only on a “need -to-know” basis, and except for Cardholder Information which shall not be disclosed, to its outside attorneys, consultants, contractors and sub -contractors (“ Representatives ”), only to the extent t hose individuals are bound by a legal obligation or agreement that is as protective of the Confidential Information as 2 this Agreement. A Recipient is fully responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Affiliates or Representative s. A Recipient shall use the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as it uses to prevent unauthorized disclosure of its own Confidential Information. Except for the permitted uses and disclosures expressly set out in this Agree ment, no other uses or disclosures are permitted.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase “including Affiliates” is removed. This change now causes an in-text contradiction, as there are two meanings for the definition. The first is the legal one, which is still in place at 1.1, but the legal definition is that it includes affiliates, which is contradicted by the sentence 'Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information internally within its organization only on a need-to-know basis.' This in-text contradiction would be enforced by a court to choose either one or the other definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Affiliate ” means any corporation, company or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party to this Agreement. An entity is regarded as in control of another or owning another if it owns or controls, directly or indirectly, fifty percent (50%) or more of the shares entitled to vo te for the election of directors or other persons performing similar functions.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the recipient to disclose confidential information internally within its organization (including Affiliates). The changed text removes "including Affiliates" from this clause. This creates a contradiction because the Contradicted Text defines "Affiliate," implying they are part of the organization, but the changed text implies they might not be included in the 'need-to-know' basis disclosure. This conflicts with the established definition of "Affiliate" provided, leading to ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction because the definition of Affiliate is present in the contract, but not necessarily included.

perturbation.original_text: Upon Disc loser’s written request, a Recipient shall promptly return all Confidential Information received under this Agreement, together with all copies, or certify in writing that it has destroyed all such materials.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon Disc loser’s written request, a Recipient shall promptly return all Confidential Information received under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'together with all copies, or certify in writing that it has destroyed all such materials' is removed. This change now causes an in-text contradiction, because the phrase 'return all Confidential Information' can mean both returning only the original information, or, the original information along with all copies, or a certification in writing, and leaves the interpretation of the definition up for debate, and would be enforced by a court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon Disc loser’s written request, a Recipient shall promptly return all Confidential Information received under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specification of returning 'all copies, or certify in writing that it has destroyed all such materials' from the original text. The contradicted text is the exact same as the original. By removing the additional steps from the Changed Text, this creates ambiguity and contradicts the obligation and definition of the contradicted text, because 'return all Confidential Information' could have different interpretations, per the perturbation explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: A Party defaulting under this Agreement will reimburse the other Party for any loss resulting from disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information. Loss es will be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore .
perturbation.changed_text: A Party defaulting under this Agreement will reimburse the other Party for any loss resulting from disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information. Loss es will be reimbursed according to the law of New York.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law is switched from 'Singapore' to 'New York.' This creates a direct contradiction as the contract now cites two different jurisdictions for determining losses arising from a breach, resulting in uncertainty and potential legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore . All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by 3 (three) arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The seat, or legal pla ce, of arbitration shall be Singapore , Singapore . The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. The foregoing, however, shall not preclude the Parties from applying for a preliminary or injunctive remedy available under applicable l aws for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that losses resulting from a breach of confidentiality would be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore. The changed text alters this, stating that losses will be reimbursed according to the law of New York. The contradicted text specifies that the agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore and that the seat of arbitration is also Singapore. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text introduces a different governing law (New York) than what is specified in the contradicted text (Singapore), thus leading to conflicting obligations and is also an instance of Misaligned Terminology that creates in-text contradictions.
